title: Azalea prinophylla
intro: Azalea prinophylla, also known as the Korean azalea, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and glossy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Azalea prinophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Azalea prinophylla is often used to make herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
